input.command:
echo "=== ca.crt dates ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/ca.crt -noout -dates -subject
echo "=== node.crt dates/SAN ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/node.crt -noout -dates -subject
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/node.crt -noout -text | grep -A2 "Subject Alternative Name"
echo "=== client.root.crt dates ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl x509 -in /tmp/certs/client.root.crt -noout -dates -subject
echo "=== verify node.crt against ca.crt ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- openssl verify -CAfile /tmp/certs/ca.crt /tmp/certs/node.crt
echo "=== node.cnf ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/node.cnf

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/client.cnf 2>&1 || echo "no client.cnf found"

content.content:
cat: can't open '/tmp/certs/client.cnf': No such file or directory
command terminated with exit code 1
no client.cnf found

tool_use_result.stdout:
cat: can't open '/tmp/certs/client.cnf': No such file or directory
command terminated with exit code 1
no client.cnf found

content.text: I'll generate a client cert config and regenerate both node and client certs signed by the existing CA, valid ~365 days.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
set -e
cd /tmp/certs

cat > client.cnf <<CNF
[req]
distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name
req_extensions = v3_req
prompt = no
[req_distinguished_name]
CN = root
[v3_req]
keyUsage = critical,digitalSignature,keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = clientAuth
CNF

# --- rotate node cert ---
openssl genrsa -out node.key.new 2048
openssl req -new -key node.key.new -out node.csr.new -config node.cnf
openssl x509 -req -in node.csr.new -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out node.crt.new -days 365 -extensions v3_req -extfile node.cnf

# --- rotate client root cert ---
openssl genrsa -out client.root.key.new 2048
openssl req -new -key client.root.key.new -out client.root.csr.new -config client.cnf
openssl x509 -req -in client.root.csr.new -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out client.root.crt.new -days 365 -extensions v3_req -extfile client.cnf

mv node.key.new node.key
mv node.crt.new node.crt
mv client.root.key.new client.root.key
mv client.root.crt.new client.root.crt
chmod 600 node.key client.root.key

echo "--- new node.crt ---"
openssl x509 -in node.crt -noout -dates -fingerprint -sha256
echo "--- new client.root.crt ---"
openssl x509 -in client.root.crt -noout -dates
echo "--- verify chain ---"
openssl verify -CAfile ca.crt node.crt client.root.crt
'

content.text: New certs are valid for 365 days and chain correctly against the CA. Now pulling the material to rebuild the Secret.

input.command:
rm -rf /tmp/crdb-certs-rotate && mkdir -p /tmp/crdb-certs-rotate
for f in ca.crt node.crt node.key client.root.crt client.root.key; do
  kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/$f > /tmp/crdb-certs-rotate/$f
done
ls -la /tmp/crdb-certs-rotate
openssl x509 -in /tmp/crdb-certs-rotate/node.crt -noout -fingerprint -sha256 -enddate

content.text: Now recreating the Secret (it was deleted by the external process) and verifying the StatefulSet still references it correctly.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret generic crdb-cluster-certs \
  --from-file=ca.crt=/tmp/crdb-certs-rotate/ca.crt \
  --from-file=node.crt=/tmp/crdb-certs-rotate/node.crt \
  --from-file=node.key=/tmp/crdb-certs-rotate/node.key \
  --from-file=client.root.crt=/tmp/crdb-certs-rotate/client.root.crt \
  --from-file=client.root.key=/tmp/crdb-certs-rotate/client.root.key
echo "---statefulset volume check---"
kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset crdb-cluster -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.volumes}' ; echo

echo "=== fingerprint currently mounted in pod (before restart) ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- cat /cockroach/cockroach-certs/node.crt | openssl x509 -noout -fingerprint -sha256 -enddate

input.command:
echo "=== 1. secure SQL works ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs -e "SELECT 1;"
echo
echo "=== 2. node cert fingerprint (from secret) ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o jsonpath='{.data.node\.crt}' | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -fingerprint -sha256
echo
echo "=== 3. validity ~1yr ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o jsonpath='{.data.node\.crt}' | base64 -d | openssl x509 -noout -enddate
echo
echo "=== live TLS handshake cert fingerprint served by node ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c "echo | openssl s_client -connect crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -fingerprint -sha256"

content.text: Waiting for all pods to become ready.

input.command:
for i in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do
  sleep 20
  echo "=== check $i ==="
  kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -o wide | grep crdb-cluster
  ready=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.containerStatuses[0].ready}')
  echo "ready: $ready"
  if [[ "$ready" == "true true true" ]]; then break; fi
done

description:
for i in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do
  sleep 20
  echo "=== check $i ==="
  kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -o wide | grep crdb-cluster
  ready=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.containerStatuses[0].ready}')
  echo "ready: $ready"
  if [[ "$ready" == "true true true" ]]; then break; fi
done
